data.title: Trump's Fed nominee on track for Senate confirmation before September meeting - report
data.description:
The Republican-controlled U.S. Senate is poised to confirm Stephen Miran, President Donald Trump's nominee to replace former Federal Reserve Governor Adriana Kugler, in time for the Federal Open Market Committee's Sept. 16-17 meeting, according to a media report on Thursday.

Democrats have privately acknowledged that they won't be able to block the confirmation, though they may be able to delay it by a few days, Bloomberg reported. The Senate Banking Committee is expected to take the matter up sometime next week.

Miran received Senate confirmation earlier this year for his role of chair of the Council of Economic Advisers. No Republicans voted against him.

In the confirmation hearings, he's likely to be pressed on his views of Federal Reserve independence and the White House's efforts to pressure the central bank to lower interest rates.

Some Republicans, who have previously defended the Fed's independence, may also question Miran about that issue.

While the GOP holds a 53-47 majority in the Senate, Trump's nominees can only afford to lose three votes. Most of the president's nominees, even controversial ones, have been confirmed in the Senate in the president's second term.

Recently, trading activity has implied an 85.2% probability of a 25-basis-point rate cut in September, up from a 75.0% chance a week ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion

data.title: Sen. Graham urges tariffs on Norway after wealth fund drops Caterpillar
data.description:
U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, a close ally of President Donald Trump, has called for new trade penalties against Norway following its sovereign wealth fund’s decision to divest from Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT).

In a social media post Thursday, the South Carolina Republican suggested the U.S. impose further tariffs on Norwegian goods and even restrict visas for executives tied to the $2 trillion wealth fund and “other organizations that attempt to punish American companies for geopolitical differences.”

Norway’s fund announced this week it was excluding Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) from its portfolio, citing the use of the company’s bulldozers in demolitions of Palestinian property in Gaza and the West Bank. As of June 30, the fund’s stake in the Texas-based machinery giant was valued at about $2.1 billion.

Washington already has a 15% tariff in place on imports from Norway while trade talks continue.

Trump has a history of reacting when congressional allies pressure U.S. companies or foreign governments. For example, he publicly called for Intel (INTC) Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan to resign after Senator Tom Cotton raised concerns about Tan’s China-linked investments. Trump later praised Tan after Intel (INTC) agreed to provide the U.S. with an ownership stake.

Norway’s sovereign wealth fund, managed by Norges Bank Investment Management, follows ethical guidelines set by parliament, with recommendations from an independent ethics council. Its exclusions range from companies tied to land mines to those involved in climate risks.

As of June 30, U.S. equities and bonds made up more than half of the fund’s portfolio, Bloomberg News reported.

data.title: 'Golden share' makes ZIM a tough takeover target, CEO Glickman says
data.description:
The Israeli government's "golden share" in ZIM Integrated Shipping (NYSE:ZIM) requires that the majority of the board are Israeli, including the Chairman and CEO, which Eli Glickman said narrows down the field of people who may be interested in the company, TradeWinds reported Thursday.

ZIM (NYSE:ZIM) President and CEO Eli Glickman declined to comment on a rumored privatization effort linking him with Israeli shipping magnate Abraham Unger, but "this is a high-risk market," he said, according to the report. "Not that many people are going to take this risk."

Glickman also noted that it took Idan Ofer, whose Kenon Holding was ZIM's (ZIM) largest shareholder until selling its 20.7% stake late last year, 20 years to recover his investment in the company after his interests took control of the company in 2004.

The restrictive provisions involved in any transfer of ZIM's (ZIM) ownership may have taken its toll in the financial markets, with shares slumping 22% since buyout rumors surfaced on August 11.

Ungar reportedly sought to buy ZIM (ZIM), take it private, then merge it with his company Ray Shipping in a cash-and-stock deal, potentially bringing in Greek investors to accomplish the transaction.

data.title: Build-A-Bear increases 2025 guidance, targets at least 60 new locations amid record Q2 results
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. (BBW) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Sharon Price John, President, CEO & Director, opened by highlighting, "we are pleased to report the best second quarter and first half results in Build-A-Bear's history, achieving record revenue while simultaneously expanding pretax margin and earnings per share." John attributed the performance to the company's focus on its three strategic pillars: a capital-light partner-operated retail model for global expansion, expanded digital transformation and social media-driven omnichannel sales, and new product initiatives like the Mini Beans collection.
  • John announced, "we are increasing our 2025 guidance at this juncture," citing strong business momentum and an improved outlook for net new unit growth.
  • J. Christopher Hurt, Chief Operations & Experience Officer, detailed that "in the quarter, 14 net new experience locations were opened, of which 86% were international," and noted expansion into 32 countries, with plans for a multilevel workshop at ICON Park in Orlando in 2026.
  • Hurt stated, "given that our 2025 expansion plans are exceeding expectations, we are increasing our net new unit growth guidance for the year to at least 60 locations from the previous 50."
  • Voin Todorovic, Chief Financial Officer, emphasized, "this was the most profitable second quarter and first half in the company's history," and reported, "during the quarter, we returned $6 million through dividends and share repurchases, bringing our year-to-date total to $13.1 million."

Outlook

  • Management increased revenue and pretax income guidance, now expecting revenue growth in the "range of mid-single to high single digits."
  • Pretax income guidance was raised to "$62 million to $70 million, which assumes the current tariff rates to be in effect for the rest of our fiscal year" (CFO Todorovic).
  • The company lifted its target for net new unit growth in 2025 to "at least 60 locations from the previous 50" (COO Hurt).
  • Management continues to expect the majority of new unit growth to be partner-operated, primarily internationally.

Financial Results

  • Total revenues for Q2 were $124.2 million, an increase of 11.1% year-over-year (CFO Todorovic).
  • Net retail sales were $114.6 million, up 10.8%, with commercial revenue growing 18.3%.
  • Gross margin was 57.6%, up 340 basis points, driven by improved merchandise margin, reduced promotional activity, and leverage of fixed costs.
  • Pretax income grew 32.7% to $15.3 million, and EPS was $0.94, an increase of 46.9%.
  • Cash and cash equivalents at quarter end totaled $39.1 million, an increase of 55.4% compared to the same period last year.
  • Inventory was $81.8 million, with about half the dollar increase attributable to tariffs.
  • The company finished the quarter with no borrowings under its revolving credit facility.

Q&A

  • Eric Martin Beder, Small Cap Consumer Research, LLC: Asked about consumer response to price increases and maturity of partner-operated stores. John explained selective and strategic price increases, focusing on value and managing entry-level price points, while Todorovic noted, "we really haven't seen negative impact of some of the selective price changes."
  • Beder inquired about the maturity and impact of partner-operated stores. Hurt responded, "as these stores start to mature, we've seen very great response from our partners as they are adding more locations."
  • Gregory Thomas Gibas, Northland Capital Markets: Asked about Mini Beans sales and wholesale opportunities. John said, "we've seen an 80% increase of Mini Beans on a year-on-year basis," and Hurt noted successful expansion of Mini Beans into international and wholesale channels, including over 50 Applegreen stores.
  • Gibas also queried e-commerce momentum. Todorovic reported a 15.1% increase in e-commerce demand and ongoing efforts to advance digital transformation and omnichannel strategy.
  • Keegan Tierney Cox, D.A. Davidson: Questioned implied guidance for the second half and partner-operated store momentum. Todorovic cited tougher comps and macro uncertainties, especially tariffs, as reasons for expected margin pressure. He pointed to a nearly $16 million headwind from tariffs and labor costs, but expressed confidence in meeting or exceeding last year's full-year profitability at the high end of guidance.
  • Steven Silver, Argus Research Company: Asked about potential for company-operated store expansion outside the U.S. Todorovic stated, "we are definitely open in considering operating stores," but current focus remains on partner-operated expansion due to ROI and local market expertise.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on tariffs, pricing, partner-operated stores, and product performance, with a slightly positive to neutral tone. Questions often highlighted challenges but acknowledged strong results and guidance increases.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing record performance and strategic discipline. During Q&A, management addressed concerns directly, with Todorovic stating, "we are doing things that's within our control," and John reiterating, "we remain confident in our continued efforts."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts maintain a confident and constructive tone, with slightly more focus on cost pressures and tariffs this quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from reiteration to an increase for both revenue and pretax income, with a higher net new unit target (60 vs. 50).
  • Strategic focus remains on global expansion, digital transformation, and leveraging brand equity, but more detail was provided on successful product launches and international partner growth.
  • Analysts this quarter pressed further on margin pressures, tariff impacts, and partner store performance, compared to a broader focus last quarter on retail channel and Mini Beans expansion.
  • Key metrics such as revenue, EPS, and gross margin improved sequentially. Management's confidence level remained high, with increased clarity on mitigation strategies for external headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tariffs as a significant headwind, with the impact expected to be less than $11 million for fiscal 2025, plus $5 million in additional labor and medical costs, totaling nearly $16 million in headwinds.
  • Tariff costs are mitigated through dual sourcing, inventory pull-forward, and leveraging international expansion.
  • Macroeconomic uncertainties and inflationary pressures remain ongoing risks, but management expressed comfort with inventory levels and flexibility in capital allocation.
  • Analysts signaled ongoing concern about margin pressure, guidance for the second half, and the sustainability of international and product-driven growth.

Final Takeaway

Build-A-Bear Workshop delivered its strongest second quarter ever, increasing revenue and pretax income guidance for 2025 while signaling at least 60 net new locations, predominantly internationally and through partner-operated models. The company credits its capital-light expansion, successful product launches like Mini Beans, and robust omnichannel strategy for driving record results despite tariff and cost headwinds. With continued investment in brand initiatives and digital transformation, management remains focused on profitable growth and shareholder returns for the remainder of the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Titan Machinery shares drop on margin pressure, weaker ag demand
data.description:
Shares of Titan Machinery (NASDAQ:TITN) fell as much as 8% on Thursday after the farm and construction equipment dealer reported fiscal second-quarter results that topped Wall Street’s estimates but highlighted persistent margin pressure and weaker demand in its core agriculture business.

The company reported a net loss of $6.0 million, or $0.26 per share, compared with a loss of $0.19 per share in the same quarter last year. On an adjusted basis, Titan (NASDAQ:TITN) posted a loss of $0.26 per share, reversing adjusted earnings of $0.17 a year earlier. Even so, the result was better than Wall Street’s forecast for a $0.48 loss.

Revenue totaled $546.4 million, down from $633.7 million a year ago but comfortably above analyst expectations of $503.8 million.

Margin pressure, agriculture weakness

Despite the beat, investors appeared focused on the underlying negatives. Gross profit margin slipped to 17.1% from 17.7% a year ago, as Titan (TITN) discounted equipment to reduce inventory in the face of sluggish farmer demand.

Management said softer commodity prices and elevated interest rates have crimped farm profitability, leading to an 18.7% same-store sales decline in its agriculture segment, which represents the bulk of its revenue. The unit swung to a $12.3 million pre-tax loss from a modest profit a year earlier.

Bryan Knutson, Titan’s (TITN) president and chief executive, said the company remains focused on its publicly stated goal of cutting $100 million in equipment inventory this fiscal year, a key initiative to lift margins and reduce floorplan interest costs.

“As far as how much we look to exceed the $100 million by — of course, the $100 million is what we're maintaining publicly as our goal,” he said in a conference call with analysts. “Internally, our goals are a lot higher than that. But there’s a lot of variables here for the rest of the year.”

The company narrowed guidance for an adjusted full-year loss of $1.50 to $2 a share from a range of $1.25 to $2 previously.

Other segments mixed

Results elsewhere were uneven. European operations posted a 38% jump in revenue, supported by stimulus programs in Romania, while construction sales fell 10% year over year. In Australia, revenue plunged nearly 49% as sprayer deliveries normalized following a backlog clearance last year.

Titan's dealer network carries brands including Case IH, Case Construction and New Holland.

data.title: Informatica gains as granted HSR earlier termination for Salesforce deal
data.description:
Informatica (NYSE:INFA) rose 1.6% after the Federal Trade Commission granted  HSR early termination for its planned sale to Salesforce (CRM).

The early termination notice was disclosed on the  antitrust regulator's website on Thursday. 

In late May, Salesforce (CRM) announced a deal to pay $25 per share  in cash for enterprise cloud software company Informatica (NYSE:INFA).

data.title: Lilly to discount Mounjaro in UK when price hike goes into effect - report
data.description:
• Eli Lilly will offer discounts off the cost of its weight loss drug Mounjaro (tirzepatide) in the UK when price hikes go into effect on Sept. 1.
  • The list price of the drug's 15mg highest dose will hit £247.50 from £122 a month, with the discount, Sky News reported. Without it, the cost would have jumped to £330.
  • Discounts are proportionate to the dosage strenfth of Mounjaro.
  • Sky News said that about 1.25M UK citizens take weight loss drugs, and 90% of them take Mounjaro.
  • "We are now aligning the list price more consistently to ensure fair global contribution to the cost of innovation," Lilly said in a statement to the news channel. 
  • On Aug. 27, Lilly said it was temporarily pausing shipments of Mounjaro to the UK to prevent stockpiling before the price hike goes into effect.

data.title: Comcast NBC Universal to offer Peacock Plus streaming service on Amazon Prime
data.description:
Comcast NBC Universal (NASDAQ:CMCSA) will now offer its Peacock Premium Plus streaming service on Amazon’s (AMZN) Prime Video platform.

Prime members can stream ad-free Peacock programs for $16.99 per month, or $169.99 annually, the same price if going directly through Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA).

The two also renewed distribution deals for access to Peacock on Amazon’s (AMZN) Fire TV devices, films from the Universal Pictures library, and Prime Video on Comcast’s (NASDAQ:CMCSA) Xfinity X1 platform. Subscribers will also have access to NBA games beginning this fall thanks to a $76M deal between the league and Comcast (CMCSA), Amazon (AMZN) and Disney (DIS).

“Adding Peacock to our growing list of subscriptions – while renewing our long-standing agreements for Fire TV, Universal Pictures Home Entertainment, and Xfinity X1 – deepens an incredible working relationship with Comcast/NBC Universal, and we look forward to a future of mutual distribution that benefits our shared customers,” said Mike Hopkins, the head of Amazon’s Prime Video and MGM Studio units in a statement.

Terms of the deal were not disclosed.

data.title: Wall Street inches higher after a slew of economic data and NVDA earnings results
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes are inching higher on Thursday, as investors weigh a wave of economic data and Nvidia’s (NVDA) latest earnings, a day after the S&P 500 secured a record close.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) finds itself up by 0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has climbed 0.1%, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) moved higher by 0.4%.

From a sector-by-sector vantage point, six of the 11 S&P segments are up in the green with Communication Services and Info Tech leading the way. On the other end, Health Care has suffered the most so far on the day. 

Shifting to the bond market, the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) has moved down 2 basis points to 4.21%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) has moved higher by 2 basis points to 3.63%.

On the economic front, Q2 GDP growth was revised higher to 3.3%, while the PCE increase was revised lower to 2.0%. 

Furthermore, initial jobless claims  unexpectedly fell in the past week. Q2 corporate profits bounced back, rising 2.0%. 

Additionally, pending home sales dipped 0.4% in July, which was weaker than economists had forecasted. 

Lisa Cook filed a lawsuit challenging President Donald Trump's move to oust her from the Federal Reserve Board of Governors, escalating the conflict between the White House and the U.S. central bank.

Nvidia (NVDA) remains in focus as shares dip 1.2% after the firm reported fiscal second-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's expectations and unveiled a massive share buyback program.

As for stocks that are on move, shares of Snowflake (SNOW) have surged 17.7% after the company produced a strong earnings report.

data.title: Gold funds are still on top from an AUM stance, but bitcoin funds are closing fast
data.description:
Assets under management in bitcoin funds are closing in on gold-backed funds, underscoring the rapid rise of cryptocurrencies as mainstream investment vehicles.

Data from Bespoke Investment Group shows bitcoin funds now hold approximately $150B in assets, compared with $180B across gold funds—a difference of just $30B. 

The narrowing gap has accelerated since the approval of spot bitcoin exchange traded funds, which have provided investors with easier access to the digital asset. This development has heightened comparisons between bitcoin (BTC-UD) and gold (XAUUSD:CUR), often viewed by some as competing safe-haven stores of value. 

Among individual funds, SPDR Gold Shares (NYSEARCA:GLD) remains the largest gold ETF with $104.16B in AUM. On the crypto side, the leading bitcoin ETF has amassed $82.68B, highlighting the speed at which bitcoin products have attracted institutional and retail capital. 

The trend also signals some form of shifting investor preferences and reinforces bitcoin’s growing role in global asset allocation. 

See the below visual chart provided by Bespoke Investment Group: 

Gold ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:GLDM), (NYSEARCA:SGOL), (NYSEARCA:IAUM), (NYSEARCA:OUNZ), (BATS:AAAU), and (NYSEARCA:BAR). 

Bitcoin ETFs: (NASDAQ:IBIT), (BATS:FBTC), (NYSEARCA:GBTC), (NYSEARCA:BTC), (BATS:ARKB), (NYSEARCA:BITO), and (BATS:HODL).
